headline: AI Chip Demand Surges, Oil Jumps on Mideast Strikes, Fed Outlook Shifts
summary: SK Hynix projects decade-long memory demand exceeding capacity, while TSMC expands CoWoS. Oil prices surged over 3% following US-Iran exchanges. The Fed's July decision looms with inflation concerns, despite some trimmed mean PCE stability.

bullets.lead: Memory demand is expected to outstrip production capacity for the next decade.

segments.text: SK Hynix CEO stated that customers are signing long-term contracts with minimum volume commitments and price floors, indicating a sustained supply shortage.

sources.title: 대만에 몰린 AI 랙 공급망…LG전자, 빅테크 새 선택지로[biz-플러스]
What Trump Voters Say About the Economy Now
Column: Compute is no longer the AI bottleneck. Memory is — and suppliers know it

segments.text: The bit exchange rate for HBM4E from commodity DRAM is estimated at 4-5x, meaning every incremental 1TB of HBM4E demand crowds out over 4TB of commodity DRAM-equivalent wafer bit output.

sources.title: Column: Compute is no longer the AI bottleneck. Memory is — and suppliers know it

bullets.lead: TSMC is aggressively expanding its advanced packaging capacity to meet AI demand.

segments.text: TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity is projected to exceed 200,000 wafers per month by 2027, with 24-hour shifts accelerating the build-out, driven by demand from NVIDIA and CSPs.
Taiwan's semiconductor exports surged in June by 33.4% YoY and in H1 2026 by 43.9%, with DRAM exports up 158.5%, driven by blockbuster demand for AI-related goods.

bullets.lead: Meta is advancing its in-house AI chip production.

segments.text: Meta's fourth-generation in-house AI chip, 'Iris,' is set for production in September, aiming to double compute capacity to 14GW next year, with Broadcom as a design partner and TSMC manufacturing.

sources.title: Meta to put AI chip into production in September as it looks to double computing capacity, memo shows

bullets.lead: Nvidia's Kyber roadmap faces manufacturing challenges.

segments.text: Nvidia removed Kyber from its roadmap due to manufacturing difficulties with the orthogonal backplane, while HDI PCB issues are bottlenecking VR PCB production.

bullets.lead: The Dallas Fed's Trimmed Mean PCE suggests underlying inflation stability.

segments.text: Excluding volatile oil prices, the Dallas Fed's April data shows the Trimmed Mean PCE stabilizing between 2-2.5%, which could support arguments for future rate cuts.

sources.title: Warsh and US Inflation Will Set Tone for July Fed Decision
War Leaves Economy With More Stubborn Inflation, Economists Project

bullets.lead: The Bank of Japan is expected to raise interest rates.

segments.text: The BOJ is projected to increase its benchmark interest rate by 25bp to 1.0% today, marking the first time Japan has returned to 1% rates since 1995, with economists anticipating further hikes to 1.25% by year-end.

segments.text: Goldman Sachs is discussing the implications of the Federal Reserve's policy outlook for equity markets.

bullets.lead: Oil prices surged following US-Iran military exchanges.

segments.text: WTI crude oil rose to $81 and Brent crude to $85, jumping over 3% after the US and Iran launched strikes in the Middle East over the weekend.

bullets.lead: Porsche anticipates continued weakness in China's luxury car market.

segments.text: Porsche warns that demand for luxury cars in China remains weak, with product gaps potentially extending until 2028.

sources.title: Porsche warns China luxury car demand remains weak, product gap may extend to 2028

bullets.lead: LG Electronics is emerging in the AI rack supply chain.

segments.text: LG Electronics is becoming a new choice for big tech companies in the AI rack supply chain, which has historically been concentrated in Taiwan.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix CEO on long-term memory shortage
x_themes.summary: SK Hynix CEO stated that memory demand will exceed production capacity for the next decade, with customers signing long-term contracts with minimum volume commitments and price floors, indicating a structural change in the memory market.

TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity is projected to reach over 200,000 wafers per month by 2027, with 24-hour shifts to accelerate build-out, driven by demand from NVIDIA and CSPs like Google and Amazon. SoIC capacity is also ramping significantly, with AMD turning to other OSATs for additional orders.

x_themes.topic: HBM bit exchange rate and DRAM supply tightening
x_themes.summary: The bit exchange rate for HBM4E from commodity DRAM is estimated at 4-5x, meaning every incremental 1TB of HBM4E demand crowds out >4TB of commodity DRAM-equivalent wafer bit output, tightening the entire DRAM complex.

x_themes.topic: Optical sector demand outlook and market sentiment
x_themes.summary: Despite short-term weakness from CPO delay reports and China capacity scares, the optical sector is expected to see rising total demand due to growing XPU numbers, with short sellers reportedly planning to close positions and buy in 2027 for CPO opportunities. VCSELs are seen as a bridge for NPO/short reach, while UHP CW is ideal for CPO.

x_themes.topic: CPO volume ramp dependencies and physical constraints
x_themes.summary: The real volume ramp for CPO is constrained by energy, heat, and reach, requiring 800V power, liquid cooling, external-laser reliability, and optical-engine qualification to be ready simultaneously.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR strong performance post-listing
x_themes.summary: SK Hynix's ADR ($SKHYV) saw a significant premium and strong performance post-listing, trading up 16%, potentially due to investors moving funds to the US instrument and different demand curves compared to its Korean listing.

x_themes.topic: Taiwan's surging semiconductor exports driven by AI
x_themes.summary: Taiwan's semiconductor exports surged in June (+33.4% YoY) and H1 2026 (+43.9%), with DRAM exports up 158.5%, driven by blockbuster demand for AI-related goods. Chip equipment and chip imports also rose significantly.

x_themes.topic: NVIDIA Kyber roadmap cancellation and VR PCB bottlenecks
x_themes.summary: NVIDIA removed Kyber from its roadmap due to manufacturing difficulties with the orthogonal backplane, while Victory Giant's HDI PCB issues (impedance mismatch) are bottlenecking Virtual Rack (VR) production, potentially causing further delays.

x_themes.topic: Meta's 'Iris' AI chip roadmap and supply chain
x_themes.summary: Meta's 4th generation in-house AI chip, 'Iris,' is set for production in September, aiming to double compute to 14GW next year. Broadcom is a design partner, TSMC will manufacture, and Meta has secured long-term supply agreements with Samsung (memory), SanDisk (flash), and Sumitomo (fiber optic).

x_themes.topic: NVIDIA's acquisition of Groq and AI ASIC competition
x_themes.summary: Jensen Huang reportedly panic-bought Groq after learning of the OpenAI-Cerebras deal, despite Groq's LPU being underwhelming, indicating NVIDIA's aggressive stance in the competitive AI ASIC market.

x_themes.topic: Apple using Intel fabs for Mac/iPhone chips due to tariff exemption
x_themes.summary: Apple reportedly secured an exemption from semiconductor tariffs by committing to manufacture Mac and iPhone chips at Intel's fabs.

x_themes.topic: Surging demand and supply constraints for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs
x_themes.summary: Demand for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs for new GPU and ASIC chips is expected to surge in 2H26, but supply chain expansion faces machinery lead times and yield issues, leading to resilient pricing and potential increases.

x_themes.topic: AI's impact on software development and entrepreneurship
x_themes.summary: AI is making software development ideas more feasible by reducing required hours and token spend, fueling the current AI build-out with near-term ROI, and setting the stage for future advancements like agents and robots.

x_themes.topic: OpenAI disrupting major tech companies with its ecosystem
x_themes.summary: Sam Altman and OpenAI are seen as disruptors to major tech companies like Apple, Google, and Microsoft by building their own ecosystem rather than being absorbed into existing ones.
